message:
feat(audio): make built-in Rust decoder the primary normalization path, ffmpeg as fallback

The audio normalization pipeline previously tried ffmpeg first and fell
back to the built-in Rust decoder (symphonia) only when ffmpeg was
unavailable. This inverts the strategy: the built-in decoder is now the
PRIMARY path for common audio formats (MP3, AAC, FLAC, WAV, OGG, ALAC),
and ffmpeg is only invoked as a FALLBACK when symphonia cannot handle the
input (video files, exotic codecs like AC3/DTS/Opus-in-MKV, etc.).

Key changes in src/audio.rs:
- Rename FORCE_BUILTIN_NORMALIZE_ENV -> FORCE_FFMPEG_NORMALIZE_ENV
  (semantics inverted: the env var now forces ffmpeg instead of forcing
  the built-in decoder, since the built-in decoder is already default)
- Restructure normalize_to_wav_with_timeout() to attempt the built-in
  decoder first, catching errors and falling through to ffmpeg
- Extract normalize_via_ffmpeg() helper for the ffmpeg subprocess path
- Add -vn flag to ffmpeg args to strip video tracks automatically,
  enabling transparent audio extraction from video files (MP4, MKV, AVI)
- Change output type from PathBuf ownership to to_path_buf() conversion
  since the output parameter is now a borrowed &Path

Key changes in tests/cli_integration.rs:
- Remove FRANKEN_WHISPER_FORCE_BUILTIN_NORMALIZE=1 from the
  transcribe_file_input_uses_builtin_normalizer_when_ffmpeg_missing test
  env vars, since the built-in normalizer is now used by default when
  PATH is empty (no explicit opt-in needed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
